editableNewDialogue.text: I’m making a set of comma-separated prompts for an image model. Can you give me 6 prompts that capture “Uranus in Sagittarius” in a mythic sci‑fi style?

cosmic archer, Uranus electric aura, Sagittarius constellation, starship horizon, nebula storm, mythic sci-fi, cinematic lighting
ancient observatory ruins, holographic star maps, lightning revelation, Sagittarius glyph, Uranus rings in sky, teal and gold palette, wide shot
dimensional portal, traveler leaping forward, cracked energy arcs, Sagittarius arrow motif, futuristic desert, surreal scale, high contrast
centaur philosopher, floating books and code, Uranian sparks, Sagittarian fire, galactic library, stained glass starlight, ultra-detailed
rebel caravan in space, solar sails, anarchic freedom theme, Uranus symbol, Sagittarius banner, cosmic dust, dynamic motion blur
oracle at crossroads, neon runes, celestial compass, Uranus storm clouds, Sagittarius bow silhouette, dreamlike haze, epic composition
Nice. Now do 6 for “Saturn in Scorpio,” but make them darker and more symbolic than literal.

obsidian temple, Saturn ring shadow, Scorpio sigil carved stone, slow dripping wax, discipline and dread, chiaroscuro, occult symbolism
sealed iron vault, scorpion emblem, hourglass dust, Saturnine restraint, underworld corridor, muted reds and blacks, minimalist
submerged catacombs, black water mirror, Saturn glyph faintly glowing, Scorpio stinger motif, ancestral secrets, fog, high detail
alchemy circle, bone and lead, Scorpio constellation in smoke, Saturn as stern judge, ritual silence, textured film grain, symbolic
thorned crown, scorpion tail silhouette, cracked marble, Saturn rings as chains, transformation through endurance, gothic lighting
graveyard of masks, hidden power dynamics, Scorpio venom flower, Saturn clockface eclipse, psychological depth, surreal, dark palette
Also: quick trivia check—Tycho Brahe’s pet moose story is real, right?

It’s a famous anecdote: Tycho Brahe is said to have had a tame moose that reportedly drank beer and later died after falling down stairs at a banquet. Historians generally treat it as plausible-but-hard-to-verify (the moose appears in later accounts rather than Tycho’s own writings), so it’s often repeated with a “legendary” asterisk.
One more: 6 prompts for “Mars opposition Uranus,” emphasizing sudden breaks, accidents, and breakthroughs—no gore.

collision of red Mars force and electric Uranus lightning, split-screen composition, sudden rupture, futuristic skyline, dramatic energy arcs
shattered chain links midair, shockwave, Mars symbol stamped steel, Uranus neon circuit patterns, freedom surge, clean minimal background
race car skidding sparks (no crash), blue lightning overhead, Mars aggression, Uranus disruption, frozen moment, cinematic motion blur
robotic arm glitching, red-hot welding flash, Uranus electric interference, Mars drive, invention born from chaos, high-tech workshop
storm over power station, breaker switch flipping, Mars heat haze, Uranus lightning, sudden blackout then bright innovation, moody lighting
athlete launching off a cracked track, electric aura, Mars adrenaline, Uranus unpredictability, breakthrough leap, surreal clean aesthetic
If you tell me which image model you’re using (Midjourney / SDXL / DALL·E, etc.), I can tune these to its preferred prompt syntax (e.g., add aspect ratio tags, style weights, negative prompts).
make the prompts look like Midjourney inputs too — short, cinematic, high contrast, with negative prompts and an aspect ratio tag

Got it — I’ll format them as Midjourney-style keyword strings with a cinematic look, high-contrast lighting, include negative prompts (like “--no text --no watermark”), and add an aspect ratio tag (e.g., “--ar 16:9”).

quick correction before we go further: Venus isn’t at 00° Taurus in this chart. My Venus is at 00° Gemini, and Lilith is at 00° Taurus — that’s why the Venus–Lilith conjunction shows up in the aspect list.

Got it — that changes the baseline positions. If your Venus is 00° Gemini and Lilith is 00° Taurus, then Venus is not in Taurus here, and the conjunction you’re referencing would be Venus conjunct Lilith only if Lilith is also at 00° Gemini (same sign/degree range). With Lilith at 00° Taurus, Venus at 00° Gemini is actually a 30° separation (a semi-sextile), not a conjunction. So for this chart we should treat the corrected placements as: Venus 00° Gemini, Lilith 00° Taurus, and re-check any aspects that depended on Venus being in Taurus.
